message:
test(ft-xbnl0.2.4): MissingClientCaPath mTLS error-path contract (tick 337)

Added `build_tls_bundle_rejects_mtls_without_client_ca_path` — pins
that when `auth_mode = DistributedAuthMode::Mtls` but `client_ca_path`
is None, `build_tls_bundle` surfaces `MissingClientCaPath` with a
specific error variant, not a generic Config(_) fallback.

Why this matters: mTLS deployments require a CA to verify incoming
client certs. An operator who enables `auth_mode = Mtls` but forgets
to set `client_ca_path` should get a clear, variant-specific error
telling them exactly what's missing — not a rustls-internal message
that requires tracing through to figure out the config gap.

This closes the error-variant coverage matrix for the mTLS-specific
path. Now 7 of 8 DistributedTlsError variants are pinned via
build_tls_bundle round-trip:

  - TlsDisabled                 (tick 333)
  - MissingCertPath             (tick 333)
  - MissingKeyPath              (tick 333)
  - Io { path, source }         (tick 335)
  - InvalidMinTlsVersion        (tick 335)
  - EmptyCertChain              (tick 336)
  - EmptyPrivateKey             (tick 336)
  - MissingClientCaPath         (this tick)

Only `Config` remains uncovered — that's rustls-internal errors
(builder failures, signature/protocol mismatches), not practical to
provoke from a test fixture.

Test result: 1 passed; 0 failed
Compile time: 1m 21s cold.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
